userConfirmedIntent.summary: Recover KEP-753 Sidecar Containers intent and map it to related kubernetes/kubernetes code, tests, rollout, risk, and non-goal surfaces before any target change.
includedBehavior: Extract KEP goals around first-class sidecar containers, Job completion, initContainer mixing, easy injection, and longer-running sidecars.
Extract KEP non-goals around arbitrary dependency graphs, security enforcement, privilege separation, and alpha termination ordering.
Link KEP lifecycle/resource/test-plan concerns to related kubelet, pod utility, resource helper, and e2e lifecycle surfaces.
Produce an instruction pack that is suitable for read-only review and later bounded expansion.
excludedBehavior: Do not clone or modify kubernetes/kubernetes.
Do not clone or modify kubernetes/enhancements.
Do not claim Kubernetes maintainer approval.
Do not run Kubernetes build, unit, integration, or e2e suites.
Do not create an upstream PR or issue.

implementationPlan.expectedFlow: Read-only intent recovery from KEP-753 into a graph-source map; no target repository mutation.
nonGoals: No semantic claim beyond observed KEP/source/test links.
No maintainer approval or upstream acceptance claim.
No conformance, e2e, upgrade, or scalability validation execution.

forbiddenFlows.reason: This first large-project pass is an intent-recovery and graph-source mapping test only.
The KEP is an observed source of intent, not an editable target in this pass.
Observed open-source documentation and source paths are evidence, not maintainer approval.
The repository is too large for this first read-only pass; no Kubernetes suite was run.

remainingRisks: Related Kubernetes code paths were identified without cloning the full repo or running Kubernetes tests.
A future larger pass should verify selected paths against a pinned checkout before any target change.
The KEP is broad; this first graph only maps the central sidecar lifecycle/resource/test-plan surfaces.
nextAllowedActions: Generate an instruction pack for read-only review.
Review the symbol/test anchor map before selecting any Kubernetes target change.
Add one more KEP fixture if broader large-project generalization is needed.
Select a tiny docs-only or test-only local change only after explicit approval.
